steps.title: Identify what the blank must express
steps.content: Scholars have long been skeptical that the horned helmets are Viking. The radiocarbon dates show the helmets are from the Nordic Bronze Age — centuries before the Vikings existed. The blank must describe what the findings do to that pre-existing skepticism: they give scholars grounds for their doubt, confirming it was reasonable. The blank needs a word meaning "confirm as reasonable."

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'anticipate': Means to expect beforehand or come before. The skepticism already existed before the research, so the findings cannot anticipate it. ✗
  • Choice B — 'inspect': Means to examine closely. Findings are inanimate and cannot inspect a scholarly attitude; the word is also semantically wrong for this context. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reveal': Means to uncover or make known. The scholars' skepticism was already publicly known, so there is nothing to reveal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'justify': Means to show or confirm as reasonable. The radiocarbon dates proving the helmets predate the Vikings by centuries give scholars solid grounds for their doubt — they justify the skepticism. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
